suite: DMTAP-PUB (§22) + CAD/artifact profile (§23) — suite 0x01 (classical): Ed25519 / BLAKE3-256
generated_by: conformance/vectors/gen_pub_vectors.py (this repo) — NOT the dmtap-core reference crate: these bytes are produced by this script alone, from the specification text. Independently cross-checked by conformance/vectors/verify_pub_vectors.py, a from-scratch second implementation of the same formulas that does not import this script. UPDATED: dmtap-core DOES implement DMTAP-PUB today (`dmtap_core::pubobj`, §22) — the earlier 'does not yet implement it' note is stale — and the Envoir conformance runner executes every vector in this file against that crate as a mandatory gate. The script-not-crate provenance therefore now means these vectors are an INDEPENDENT check on the implementation rather than a restatement of it.
methodology: All values computed from FIXED seeds/inputs by this script; no randomness, no wall-clock reads. Ed25519 is deterministic (RFC 8032); BLAKE3-256 and CBOR are deterministic. CBOR here is the same §18-canonical, integer-keyed (COSE/CWT-style) deterministic encoding (RFC 8949 §4.2) used by conformance/vectors/vectors.json — a second implementer following §18.1.1/§18.1.2 and §22 alone reproduces these bytes without running this script.

vectors.name: pub_manifest_single_chunk
vectors.note: PubManifest.id over a single plaintext chunk (n=1): MTH([h_0]) = leaf(h_0), leaf(h)=BLAKE3-256(DS||0x00||h), DS='DMTAP-PUB-v0/manifest'||0x00 (§22.2.2). id = 0x1e || MTH(...).

vectors.name: pub_manifest_three_chunks
vectors.note: PubManifest.id over 3 ordered plaintext chunks (n=3, RFC 6962 split k=2): MTH(h0,h1,h2) = node(node(leaf(h0),leaf(h1)), leaf(h2)), DS-tag folded into every leaf/node (§22.2.2).

vectors.name: pub_manifest_type_incompatibility
vectors.note: Same ordered chunk-hash list (h0,h1,h2 from pub_manifest_three_chunks), rooted two ways: 'public_root' folds DS-tag 'DMTAP-PUB-v0/manifest'||0x00 into every leaf/node (§22.2.2); 'sealed_style_root' uses the §18.9.5 bare tree (leaf=BLAKE3-256(0x00||h), node=BLAKE3-256(0x01||l||r), no DS fold) over the identical h_i list. The two MUST be different values (§22.2.3 type-incompatibility) — demonstrates the DS-tag alone prevents a sealed/public root collision even before considering that real sealed h_i are additionally over ciphertext, not plaintext.

vectors.name: pub_manifest_key5_forbidden

input.derivation: the pub_manifest_single_chunk PubManifest (keys 1,2,3,4,6) with a forbidden key 5 (32 zero bytes) inserted in sorted position

vectors.note: §22.2.1: a PubManifest MUST NOT carry key 5 (no AEAD, no per-file key by construction). A decoder that finds key 5 MUST reject, never use it.

vectors.name: pub_announce_signing_preimage
vectors.note: §22.3.1: PubAnnounce.sig = Ed25519(signer_seed).sign(DS || det_cbor(PubAnnounce minus key 9)), DS='DMTAP-PUB-v0/announce'||0x00. msg_hex is det_cbor of the genesis announce A0 (v=0,suite=1,pub=PK_A,roots=[pub_manifest_single_chunk.id],meta={},ts=1700000050000,signer=PK_A), keys 1,2,3,4,5,7,8 ascending (key 6 supersedes absent; key 9 sig excluded from the preimage by construction).

vectors.name: pub_announce_id
vectors.note: §22.3.1: announce_id = 0x1e || BLAKE3-256(det_cbor(PubAnnounce)) over the COMPLETE, SIGNED object (including key 9 sig) — the derived-anchor rule of §18.9.4, applied to announce A0 above (full CBOR bytes = det_cbor with keys 1,2,3,4,5,7,8,9 ascending).

vectors.name: pub_announce_supersede_same_author_valid

expected.note: successor.pub == predecessor.pub — a valid same-author revision

vectors.note: §22.3.3 step 5, §22.3.4: announce A1 (publisher A) supersedes announce A0 (also publisher A) — same-author, MUST be accepted as a valid revision link.

vectors.name: pub_announce_supersede_cross_author_invalid
vectors.note: §22.3.3 step 5, §22.3.4: announce B0 (publisher B) attempts to supersede announce A0 (publisher A) — different pub — MUST be rejected; a publisher may only supersede its own announcements.

vectors.name: pub_feed_entry_chain
vectors.note: §22.4.1 prev-chain: entry0 (seq=0, genesis, no prev) -> entry1 (seq=1, prev=entry0_id) -> entry2 (seq=2, prev=entry1_id). FeedEntry_id = 0x1e || BLAKE3-256(det_cbor(FeedEntry)) (generic §2.2 rule; see NOTE above — §22.4.1 does not spell this formula out explicitly the way §22.3.1 does for announce_id). A verifier walks prev from a signed FeedHead.tip and checks each link.

vectors.name: pub_feed_head_signing_preimage
vectors.note: §22.4.1: FeedHead.sig = Ed25519(signer_seed).sign(DS || det_cbor(FeedHead minus key 8)), DS='DMTAP-PUB-v0/feed'||0x00. FeedHead: v=0,suite=1,pub=PK_A,seq=1,tip=entry1_id (from pub_feed_entry_chain),ts=1700000051500,signer=PK_A. Because entry1.prev=entry0_id, signing tip=entry1_id transitively commits to entry0 too (§22.4.1's 'tip transitively commits to the entire log').

vectors.name: pub_feed_rollback_strict_less_than
vectors.note: §22.4.2: a reader that has already accepted seq=1 for this pub MUST reject a FeedHead presenting seq=0 (strictly less than) — stale-replay / suppression.

vectors.name: pub_feed_equal_seq_identical_tip_idempotent

expected.note: equal seq, identical tip: idempotent re-fetch, not an error

vectors.note: §22.4.2: equal seq is NOT a rollback. Re-presenting the SAME tip at the SAME seq (a reader legitimately re-fetching a cacheable FeedHead) MUST be accepted as a no-op.

vectors.name: pub_feed_equal_seq_different_tip_fork
vectors.note: §22.4.2: two distinct FeedEntry values at the SAME seq=1 (entry1_id from pub_feed_entry_chain vs entry1_alt_id here, both with prev=entry0_id) is equivocation/fork — the author's own log was rewritten. Handled HALT_ALERT, the same posture as a committer fork (0x0404) / cluster-journal break (0x0412).

vectors.name: pub_feed_genesis_carries_prev_malformed

input.derivation: the genesis entry0 (seq=0) with a `prev` field (key 3) wrongly present

vectors.note: §22.4.1: `prev` is present for every entry EXCEPT genesis (seq=0); a genesis entry carrying one is malformed.

vectors.name: pub_feed_nongenesis_missing_prev_malformed
vectors.note: §22.4.1: a non-genesis entry (seq != 0) lacking `prev` is malformed.
